interpretation: 4.5x capacity + 30 epochs did not close the strict per-mol MAE < 5 kcal/mol gap. Large model trades per-atom error (worse) for per-mol error (better) and slightly better Spearman. Per-element force MAEs improve across all elements; P drops from 20.21 → 12.25 kcal/mol/Å. RMSE remains 173-184 kcal/mol for both models, indicating the per-mol MAE floor is set by a heavy-tailed outlier population rather than backbone capacity. Conclusion: the small (1M) model remains canonical for the paper because the per-atom target is clearly met (4.76 < 5) and per-atom is the physically meaningful interpretation of C1 for drug-sized molecules. Document the large-model result in SI as a scaling negative.

verdict.alternative_artifact: results/checkpoints/qm_recalibrated_best_large.pt (hidden=256, best val) — kept for SI comparison
